recent_lessons: Rudy Gobert REB T8 missed (actual 7, one short of threshold) in a CLOSE game (SAS won by 7). The post-game news confirms he played 31 minutes through a pre-existing left rib sprain with no exit or restriction. The Analyst correctly applied the career playoff REB CAUTION (worsening trajectory -6.7pp at T6, monotonic_down) and stepped from T10 to T8 on the 25th-percentile gate. The miss by exactly 1 rebound in 31 minutes with a confirmed rib sprain is at the boundary of variance and a minor structural mechanism — the rib sprain was a known pre-existing condition that may have slightly reduced his physicality on contested boards without causing an exit. The Analyst's 72% confidence (floor) appropriately reflected this stack of risk factors.

Jalen Duren REB T8 missed badly (4 actual vs 8 threshold, -4 margin) in a CLOSE game (DET lost by 7) with no confirmed injury exit and full 29 minutes played. The post-game news explicitly confirms 'a simply poor rebounding performance.' The competitive=66% signal in the tier_walk was the only structural warning present — the analyst noted this as 'a significant concern' but proceeded to 78% confidence after a net adjustment. The miss highlights that competitive=66% on a REB pick, even with strong series form (2/2 T8), warrants a more aggressive confidence reduction or tier step than was applied.

Wembanyama ejection (G4, SAS @ MIN): Both Wembanyama picks (PTS T15, REB T10) missed because he was ejected in Q2 for a Flagrant 2 foul and played only 12 minutes. This is an unforeseeable in-game disciplinary event — not a pre-game injury, not a selection error, and not predictable from any quant signal available at pick time. The player was fully healthy and not listed on the injury report. Both misses are classified as injury_event.

VJ Edgecombe PTS T10 miss (PHI vs NYK, blowout loss): Edgecombe exited with a low back injury and did not return. He scored 8 points on 4-14 shooting before leaving — the miss was driven by both the early exit and cold shooting. The post_game_event field confirms injury_exit. Despite the morning amendment correctly identifying upside (revised to 84%) after Anunoby was ruled out, the real risk was an in-game structural exit, not the Anunoby absence. Classified as injury_event.

Paul George PTS T10 miss (PHI vs NYK, blowout loss): George played only 27 minutes in a 144-114 blowout loss and scored 7 points on 3-7 FG. The game went to blowout for NYK, and PHI's starters — including George — were benched in the fourth quarter. The pick was made at 70% confidence after a FG_MARGIN_THIN step from T15→T10; the competitive=61% warning in the tier_walk was a real signal that the game script could suppress his output. The blowout game-script was the proximate cause: George's 27-minute load was directly compressed by the 30-point NYK margin. The pre-game spread was only 1.5 points — the system had no blowout signal at pick time — but a spread of 1.5 combined with a desperate home PHI team and competitive=61% created structural fragility. Classified as model_gap_rule: the system correctly flagged competitive=61% as a warning but did not translate it into a hard skip or mandatory step-down for secondary scorers on the losing side when the game tips into blowout territory.

Jalen Duren REB T6 missed badly (2 actual vs. T6 pick) in a CLOSE game (DET lost by 9, 103-112). The game script was not a blowout — Duren played 27 minutes. The miss appears to be a performance outlier: no injury exit, full availability, but only 2 rebounds in 27 minutes. The analyst's tier_walk correctly identified competitive=64% at T8 as a significant concern and correctly flagged the 25th-pct gate, stepping to T6 and applying -16pp in total penalties. Despite reaching 72% confidence after heavy discounting, the outcome was a catastrophic 2-rebound night. The series T6 rate of 2/3 (67%) below the 70% threshold was a warning sign the analyst flagged but could not formally reject the pick on at T6. The LAL suppressor and CLE interior rebounding competition were structural suppressors that the competitive=64% signal partially captured but could not fully price in.

Jarrett Allen PTS T10 missed by 1 (9 actual vs. T10 pick, market line 9.5 — this is an exact-threshold miss under the grading rule: actual must be ≥ pick_value, and 9 < 10 makes this a miss). The analyst correctly identified vs_tough=56% as a material downgrade signal and applied a -5pp tough defense penalty. The 9-point output in 32 minutes confirms the tough defense signal was real. Web recap confirms a lingering knee issue from the regular season with no mid-game exit — the knee context may have subtly compressed his interior scoring without triggering a formal injury classification.

LeBron James AST T6 missed significantly (3 actual vs. T6 pick). The game was CLOSE (OKC won by 5, 115-110), so blowout game-script is not a causal explanation. LeBron played a full 40 minutes. The web recap notes he appeared to aggravate a hip injury from Game 2, which may have suppressed his playmaking without triggering a mid-game exit. The analyst correctly applied the VOLATILE T6+ penalty (-8pp) and trend=down signal, reaching 80% confidence (then noted as 74% in the pick object). The series 3/3 at T6 was the primary supporting signal — but a series sample of 3 games is small, and the analyst's own tier_walk noted series 1/3 at T8, implying T6 had only a thin margin. The hip injury context, while not formally listed on the injury report, was documented in the post-game news and represents a structural suppressor of playmaking that the system had no mechanism to price pre-game.

Randle AST T2 missed in a 29-point blowout loss: Randle recorded only 1 assist across 36 minutes as MIN trailed badly. The post-game recap explicitly confirms the blowout game script compressed his playmaking role — he focused on scoring and rebounding rather than facilitating. The series rate (3/4 = 75%) and overall rate (9/10 = 95%) were both favorable but neither predicted the specific mechanism: in a road blowout loss where the primary team-level offensive structure collapses, even a team's PF/power forward playmaker sees assist opportunities dry up when the defense is not competing to be set up. The CLV warning (ast_lost_close, -1.4pp live CLV movement, observed from morning through afternoon) was present and flagged but not applied. The analyst noted this as 'applied: false' — this was a missed signal.

Gobert REB T8 missed badly in a 29-point blowout: Gobert posted only 5 rebounds in 23 minutes. The post-game recap confirms two compounding factors: (1) blowout game script materially reduced his floor time (23 min vs. a projected 35+ baseline), and (2) a noted left rib sprain may have reduced his physical aggressiveness at the rim. The career playoff REB CAUTION (trajectory monotonic_down, -6.7pp at T6) was correctly applied to bring confidence to the 72% floor, but the pre-game spread of 9.5 points — which in playoffs is treated as below the blowout-penalty threshold of >11 — ultimately understated the actual blowout risk. The final margin of 29 points far exceeded what a 9.5-point spread implied, and MIN's rebounding corps was unable to compete at normal rates once the game was out of reach.
recent_reinforcements: Tier step-downs driven by FG_MARGIN flags continued to perform well: Cunningham PTS (T20→T15), Mitchell PTS (T20→T15), and Holmgren PTS (T15→T10) all hit comfortably, with Mitchell posting 43 actual vs. a T15 pick. The step-down mechanic is correctly absorbing FG efficiency uncertainty while preserving qualified picks.

Mobley's three-prop sweep (PTS T10 HIT 17, REB T4 HIT 8, AST T2 HIT 5) validates the auditor-recommended DET step-down on REB (T6→T4) and the hard gate skip on AST T4 (C-position, tough defense) in favor of T2. The multi-prop approach on a reliable player with correctly tiered picks is the right template.

Harden AST T4 hit 11 actual in a mid-series (G4) context, directly confirming the 'AST bulletproof in mid-series games' dossier signal. The analyst correctly did not apply the LATE_FADER penalty (which fires G5-7 only) and correctly held T4 over T6 given the series 2/3 at T6 rate. Dossier-derived AST stability calls are calibrated correctly for mid-series games.

Ausar Thompson REB T4 hit exactly at threshold (4 actual vs. T4 pick), validating the 25th-pct gate step from T6 to T4 as the correct conservative anchor. The consistent tag and 95% overall at T4 were the right primary signals; the gate step was precautionary and appropriate given uncertainty about the 3rd-lowest L10 floor.

Reaves AST T6 hit exactly at threshold (6 actual vs. T6 pick) in the LAL elimination context, validating the expanded creation role argument with Doncic OUT and the series 3/3 supporting signal. The analyst correctly held T6 despite the season H2H being only 1/3, weighting this-series evidence over stale season data — the correct playoff methodology.

Wembanyama blowout exemption validated in practice: Wembanyama posted 27 PTS and 17 REB in 33 minutes of a 29-point SAS blowout win, clearing both his PTS T15 (actual 27) and REB T8 (actual 17) picks comfortably. The directive to exempt him from blowout secondary-scorer skips is structurally confirmed — his counting-stat floors hold even in garbage-time-shortened blowout contexts. Continue applying the exemption without hesitation.

Tier walk-down discipline on SAS player PTS picks paid off across the slate: Castle PTS walked from T15 to T10 via FG_MARGIN_NEG step, Vassell PTS walked from T15 to T10 via FG_MARGIN_THIN step, and Wembanyama PTS held at T15 with a series-discount penalty. All three hit comfortably (17, 12, and 27 actual respectively vs. T10/T10/T15 thresholds). The conservative step-down cascade on FG margin flags is correctly calibrated for playoff blowout environments — it reduces tier risk without sacrificing pick count.

Castle AST T4 iron-floor pick with Fox QUESTIONABLE correctly identified upside risk redistribution: The afternoon lineup agent revised confidence upward to 91% upon Fox's QUES status, and Castle delivered 6 AST in 27 minutes (clearing T4 by 2). The playmaking-redistribution logic — Castle absorbs primary creation duties when Fox is limited — is well-supported and should continue to be applied as a systematic upward revision trigger when the primary PG is out or questionable on the same roster.

Randle REB career playoff CAUTION directive correctly steered the tier walk to T4 rather than T6: Randle posted 10 actual rebounds, clearing T4 (threshold 4) easily. Though the pick was ultra-conservative relative to actual output, the CAUTION signal on Randle REB (trajectory monotonic_down, -30.1pp at T6) was applied correctly — T6 would have been a miss given the 29-point blowout context (23 minutes for Gobert, role compression across the MIN roster in losing effort). The rule protected against a T6 pick that was genuinely at risk in this game script.

Edwards AST iron-floor T2 held in a 29-point blowout road loss: Edwards posted exactly 2 AST in 39 minutes, hitting the T2 threshold exactly (actual = pick_value = HIT per grading rules). The iron-floor designation on AST T2 for a primary scorer/ball-handler correctly identifies that even in a lopsided loss where offensive rhythm is disrupted, elite-usage players accumulate at least 2 assists across 39 minutes. This floor pattern remains reliable and the soft_floor downgrade logic (PTS/AST ratio check) was appropriately applied without overcorrecting.
recent_recommendations: WEMBANYAMA TOP_PICK RESTRICTION IN ROAD GAMES WITH PHYSICAL MATCHUP CONTEXT — procedural note: Both Wembanyama picks (PTS T15 as top_pick=true, REB T10 as top_pick=false) were structurally sound selections invalidated by ejection. The top_pick=true designation on his PTS pick contributed to him being a featured selection on this slate. Going forward, apply top_pick=false to Wembanyama PTS/REB picks in road playoff games where the opponent (MIN) has elite interior physicality (Gobert, McDaniels, Reid) and the series has already established a pattern of physical play. The series diary noted Wembanyama received a concussion in G2 from a hard fall — the physical series context was a documented risk. This is a minor procedural guard, not a tier change.

For Jalen Duren REB specifically: the series T6 rate of 2/3 (67%) fell below the 70% required threshold, but the pick was held at T6 because T4 had no market. When the only available tier has a series hit rate below the 70% minimum floor AND competitive rate below 65%, apply an additional hard skip evaluation: if the series hit rate at the selected tier is < 70% AND no lower market exists, classify as merit_below_floor and skip. The current rule only checks overall season rate against the floor — extend the floor check to also gate on series rate when series sample ≥ 3 games. This would have flagged Duren REB T6 (series 2/3 = 67% < 70%) as a skip candidate.

For Jarrett Allen (and similar secondary scorers) against tough defenses: vs_tough=56% is among the lowest matchup-specific rates in the dataset. When a secondary scorer's vs_tough rate falls below 60% at the selected tier AND the player carries a VOLATILE tag, require a mandatory additional step-down of one tier (beyond any FG_MARGIN step already applied). If no lower tier market exists, apply the merit_below_floor skip. This tighter rule specifically targets secondary scorer PTS props where tough defense creates a double suppression that the current -5pp cap does not fully price.

For LeBron James AST picks in Game 4+ of a playoff series: the trend=down signal was present and the analyst correctly applied the VOLATILE T6+ penalty. However, the series-based evidence (3/3 at T6) was given too much weight given it was only a 3-game sample in a series where LeBron's minutes and health are actively managed. Add a rule: when a player has a documented within-series injury event (hip, knee, oblique — any non-fatal injury flagged in post-game news from a prior game in the same series), apply an additional -5pp penalty to AST picks at T6+, even if not formally listed as QUESTIONABLE. The post-game news for the Game 2 hip aggravation was available in the series diary context; the afternoon injury agent should be instructed to check series diary injury language as a supplemental injury signal.

The 76-80% confidence band performed at 100% (6/6) on this slate, significantly outperforming the 78% expected rate. While one slate is insufficient to recommend raising picks in this band, this reinforces that the current penalty structure (VOLATILE -3pp, FG_MARGIN step-downs, tough defense discounts) is producing well-calibrated outputs in the 76-80% range. Do not tighten minimum confidence thresholds in this band — the current calibration is correct. The 70-75% band (72.7% actual vs. 72.5% expected) is also near-perfectly calibrated; maintain current floors.

For any REB prop where the analyst's tier_walk shows competitive rate < 65% at the primary tier AND the 25th-pct gate forces a step-down AND no lower market exists: require the pick to meet a 75% minimum series hit rate at the held tier (not the standard 70% overall floor) before proceeding. Duren REB T6 had series 2/3 (67%) — below a 75% series minimum that would have been a skip signal under this rule. This specifically addresses the scenario where competitive suppression, gate-forced holds, and thin series data combine to create a fragile pick with no downward escape.

Implement a CLV warning application rule for AST picks: The Randle AST T2 pick had an active clv_warning (ast_lost_close, -1.4pp live CLV movement, proposed_penalty_pp of 5pp) that was flagged but not applied (applied: false). For AST picks in blowout-risk game scripts (spread_abs ≥ 8.0 in playoffs), require that any CLV warning with type 'ast_lost_close' and live_clv_pp ≤ -1.0 trigger the proposed penalty automatically. A 5pp reduction on Randle AST T2 would have brought confidence from 80% to 75% — still a valid pick, but flagged as marginal and potentially below any parlay threshold, reducing exposure to this game-script-sensitive outcome.

Apply an enhanced blowout-minutes floor for road underdogs facing a spread of 8–11 points in playoffs: The 9.5-point spread threshold currently sits just below the playoff blowout-penalty gate (>11). However, the actual MIN @ SAS margin was 29 points — the spread materially underestimated the game outcome. For road underdog players with REB or AST picks where the implied spread is 8–11, apply a -5pp pre-emptive game-script risk penalty (in addition to any VOLATILE or series penalties) to account for the realistic tail of game outcomes where the actual margin far exceeds the line. This would have reduced Gobert REB T8 from 72% (floor) to 67%, triggering a skip below the 72% REB floor minimum.

For rebounding picks on road underdog players flagged with career playoff REB CAUTION AND a VOLATILE tag, require the 25th-percentile gate to clear with 3rd-lowest > pick_value + 1 (not just > pick_value): Gobert's T8 pick passed the 25th-pct gate on the narrowest possible reading (3rd-lowest estimated at ~9, gate 8 < 9). When a player carries both the career playoff REB CAUTION directive and the VOLATILE tag, the gate should require a 1-unit buffer above the pick threshold to protect against the additional tail risk introduced by those two compounding signals. Under this rule, T8 would have failed (needing 3rd-lowest ≥ 10) and the walk would have proceeded to T6 (series 4/4 = 100%, estimated 3rd-lowest=9 > 6), which still hits in this game at 5 actual — but the T6 pick would also have missed. Given both the CAUTION and VOLATILE signals, the correct outcome per existing rules would have been a skip at all tiers below 72% floor post-VOLATILE deduction. Formalize this dual-signal skip explicitly.

Add an explicit road-underdog AST suppression modifier for players whose primary role is scoring/rebounding (PTS/AST raw ratio > 5x) in blowout-risk game scripts: Randle's PTS/AST ratio this series was approximately 17 PTS / 1.25 AST = ~13.6x, well above the 5x threshold. For players with PTS/AST > 5x in a road underdog context with spread_abs ≥ 8, apply an additional -8pp penalty to AST picks, reflecting that when the game script goes badly for the road team, high-ratio scorers stop generating playmaking opportunities first. This modifier is distinct from the existing series-discount and would have reduced Randle AST T2 confidence from 80% to 72% — at the floor, triggering additional scrutiny before emission.
Playoff calibration divergence in 86+% band: 66.7% actual vs 87.5% regular-season (-20.8pp, n=6). Investigate whether this is structural (playoff defense, scheme adaptation) or variance.
